greeting: The chainman stands too still for a working man. His eyes move from your face to your pockets and back again.
shrug: "Mr. Gantt does not pay me for guesses."
deflect: "That is not a safe question in a small town."

entries.text: "A chainman. A man paid to hold one end of a line and not ask who profits from the other."
"I carry chain for the surveyor. Lately I carry messages too. Some are heavier."
"Mr. Gantt says curiosity spoils clean work. He also says you are making mud where there was a line."
"The quarry is private ground, however public the mountain looks from the road."
"Brass pins are stubborn things. Men who lean on paper dislike brass pins."
